purpose: Current-phase task checklist generated from the agent-maintained source coverage matrix and platform capability check. Use it before editing and update the matrix/check after work.
instructions: Start with implementationGroups as the current phase implementation contract: implement each group, then use mustComplete entries as exact row-level checks.

Use planContract as upstream planning ownership evidence; resolve its alignmentFindings by updating plannedPhase/matrix/platform evidence or by documenting sourceOverride/analysisGap when source inspection disagrees.
Keep grouped business behavior discoverable through precise target endpoints; avoid closing unrelated features only through root/shell/whole-file endpoints.
Merge source files only when it improves target clarity and still preserves per-feature evidence, tests, and controllable file size.
Treat mustComplete entries as the current phase's executable row-level task list.
Use behaviorAudit as the current phase behavior audit; it checks closed critical/high rows for source behavior, target behavior, parity, and verification evidence.

Use uiContract entries as static-analysis planning aids, not complete UI specifications: preserve listed source-visible screen structure, route/back behavior, actions, state feedback, and visual evidence needs, then re-read source/resources/navigation for UI behavior the static analysis may have missed.
Do not close the phase while current-phase critical/high entries are open, weakly deferred, or missing evidence.
For every closed critical/high source feature, record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence in source-coverage-matrix.json.
A build pass alone is not per-feature verification; cite tests, manual/emulator evidence, or explicit verificationEvidence for behavior closure.
Use implemented-simplified only with concrete simplification reason, preserved-behavior explanation, target endpoints or tests, and source/platform evidence.

If a suitable HarmonyOS/Cangjie API, Kit, example, or package exists for a platform capability, use it or record a platform replacement; do not close the item with local mock data, sample data, in-memory simulation, or another fallback.
Use deferred only for concrete blockers; otherwise implement faithfully or platform-replace with evidence.
Update source-coverage-matrix.json/md and platform-capability-check.json/md before phase completion.
If this checklist is empty but the phase has real scope in plan.md/plan.json, assign plannedPhase values first.

deferredOrFallback.featureName: Image viewing, bitmap/exif handling, zoom, preview, or gallery display behavior.
deferredOrFallback.featurePoint: Image viewing, bitmap/exif handling, zoom, preview, or gallery display behavior.
deferredOrFallback.sourceEvidence: path=app/src/main/java/com/example/foodike/presentation/profile/Profile.kt; category=kotlin-source; importance=critical; keyMethods=Profile; featurePoint=Image viewing, bitmap/exif handling, zoom, preview, or gallery display behavior.
deferredOrFallback.sourceBehavior: `Profile.kt` only renders the packaged profile avatar with static user text; it does not open an image viewer, decode bitmaps, inspect EXIF metadata, or support zoom/preview gestures.
deferredOrFallback.targetBehavior: `renderProfileRoute` preserves the same packaged avatar presentation through `Image(mediaProfile)` and does not introduce any gallery, zoom, or metadata workflow on the HarmonyOS profile route.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed `Profile.kt` uses only `painterResource(R.drawable.ic_profile)` for a static profile image and has no image click/viewer path.
Manual target review confirmed `profile_page.cj::renderProfileRoute` renders `mediaProfile` as a static avatar only.

covered.featureName: Navigation surface, menu, drawer, toolbar, or tab command behavior.
covered.featurePoint: Navigation surface, menu, drawer, toolbar, or tab command behavior.
covered.sourceEvidence: path=app/src/main/java/com/example/foodike/presentation/profile/Profile.kt; category=kotlin-source; importance=critical; keyMethods=Profile; featurePoint=Navigation surface, menu, drawer, toolbar, or tab command behavior.
covered.sourceBehavior: `Profile.kt` renders a back command, static profile action cards, and a logout button. The back arrow calls `navController.navigateUp()`, the action cards keep placeholder click handlers, and logout clears the start destination stack before routing to onboarding.
covered.targetBehavior: `bootstrap_page.cj::buildProfileRoute` wires `renderProfileRoute` with a translated back action to the history route, placeholder toast actions for edit and section taps, and `performLogout()` to clear route-local state and navigate back to onboarding after session logout.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed `Profile.kt` owns the back icon, edit/action-card click surfaces, and logout navigation callback.

Manual target review confirmed `bootstrap_page.cj::buildProfileRoute`, `profile_page.cj::renderProfileRoute`, and `profile_store.cj` preserve the same command surface and explicit placeholder actions.

Phase-06 build gate PASS: `translation-inputs/build-reports/phase-06-profile-capabilities-verification/build-report.md`.

covered.featureName: Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
covered.featurePoint: Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
covered.sourceEvidence: path=app/src/main/java/com/example/foodike/presentation/profile/Profile.kt; category=kotlin-source; importance=critical; keyMethods=Profile; featurePoint=Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
covered.sourceBehavior: `Profile.kt` composes against `ProfileViewModel` callbacks and navigates immediately from user actions, while keeping the visible profile identity and placeholder cards static until logout is requested.
covered.targetBehavior: `bootstrap_page.cj` owns the route-local profile callbacks and passes them into `renderProfileRoute`, while `profile_store.cj` centralizes the static identity and placeholder-action message so the translated profile route updates immediately for logout and placeholder taps without inventing new backend state.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed the profile screen is driven by callback wiring rather than editable form state or remote data streams.
Manual target review confirmed `buildProfileRoute` wires the translated profile callbacks through `renderProfileRoute` and `profile_store.cj`.
Phase-06 test build report PASS: `translation-inputs/test-build-reports/phase-06-profile-capabilities-verification/test-build-report.md` ran `cjpm test --target aarch64-linux-ohos` successfully.
Phase-06 build gate PASS: `translation-inputs/build-reports/phase-06-profile-capabilities-verification/build-report.md`.

covered.featureName: Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
covered.featurePoint: Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
covered.sourceEvidence: path=app/src/main/java/com/example/foodike/presentation/profile/ProfileViewModel.kt; category=kotlin-source; importance=critical; classes=ProfileViewModel; keyMethods=onEvent; featurePoint=Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
covered.sourceBehavior: `ProfileViewModel.onEvent` propagates the logout action by toggling login state and then invoking the navigation callback, so the profile screen reacts immediately to the logout event.
covered.targetBehavior: `performLogout()` and `profile_store.cj::logout` preserve the same immediate session-update and route-handoff behavior, while `resolveStartRoute(false)` preserves the resulting onboarding entry state after logout.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed `ProfileViewModel.kt` performs a single logout state mutation followed by the supplied callback.
Manual target review confirmed `performLogout()` executes the translated logout mutation and immediate route transition without adding extra async state layers.

covered.featureName: Screen lifecycle, entry point initialization, resume/pause refresh, or teardown behavior.
covered.featurePoint: Screen lifecycle, entry point initialization, resume/pause refresh, or teardown behavior.
covered.sourceEvidence: path=app/src/main/java/com/example/foodike/presentation/profile/ProfileViewModel.kt; category=kotlin-source; importance=critical; classes=ProfileViewModel; keyMethods=onEvent; featurePoint=Screen lifecycle, entry point initialization, resume/pause refresh, or teardown behavior.
covered.sourceBehavior: `ProfileViewModel` has no independent load/resume teardown path; its lifecycle responsibility is limited to servicing the logout event while the profile screen is active.
covered.targetBehavior: `bootstrap_page.cj::performLogout` owns the translated route teardown by clearing login and shell-local state before navigating to onboarding, while `resolveStartRoute(false)` preserves the post-logout entry behavior.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed `ProfileViewModel.kt` contains no initialization or resume logic beyond logout handling.
Manual target review confirmed `performLogout()` clears translated route-local state and `resolveStartRoute(false)` returns the onboarding route.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: presentation/profile
requiredBehaviors: `Profile.kt` only renders the packaged profile avatar with static user text; it does not open an image viewer, decode bitmaps, inspect EXIF metadata, or support zoom/preview gestures.

`Profile.kt` renders a back command, static profile action cards, and a logout button. The back arrow calls `navController.navigateUp()`, the action cards keep placeholder click handlers, and logout clears the start destination stack before routing to onboa...

`Profile.kt` composes against `ProfileViewModel` callbacks and navigates immediately from user actions, while keeping the visible profile identity and placeholder cards static until logout is requested.
`Profile.kt` uses only packaged resources, local click handlers, and navigation callbacks; it does not request runtime permissions or access protected Android APIs.
`ProfileViewModel.onEvent` propagates the logout action by toggling login state and then invoking the navigation callback, so the profile screen reacts immediately to the logout event.
`ProfileViewModel.onEvent` only toggles persisted login state and invokes the logout callback; it does not request permissions or call protected APIs.
`ProfileViewModel` has no independent load/resume teardown path; its lifecycle responsibility is limited to servicing the logout event while the profile screen is active.
`ProfileEvent.kt` defines only the `PerformLogout` callback payload and does not represent permission requests, results, or protected API access.
`ProfileCard.kt` is a clickable text-and-divider row and does not request permissions or access protected APIs.

featureContracts.featureName: Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
featureContracts.sourceBehavior: `Profile.kt` composes against `ProfileViewModel` callbacks and navigates immediately from user actions, while keeping the visible profile identity and placeholder cards static until logout is requested.
featureContracts.targetRequiredBehavior: `bootstrap_page.cj` owns the route-local profile callbacks and passes them into `renderProfileRoute`, while `profile_store.cj` centralizes the static identity and placeholder-action message so the translated profile route updates immediately for logout and placeholder taps without inventing new backend state.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed the profile screen is driven by callback wiring rather than editable form state or remote data streams.
Manual target review confirmed `buildProfileRoute` wires the translated profile callbacks through `renderProfileRoute` and `profile_store.cj`.

featureContracts.featureName: Runtime permission request, permission result handling, or protected API access.
featureContracts.sourceBehavior: `ProfileViewModel.onEvent` only toggles persisted login state and invokes the logout callback; it does not request permissions or call protected APIs.
featureContracts.targetRequiredBehavior: `profile_store.cj::logout` only delegates to the login preferences service and remains permission-free in the HarmonyOS target.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed `ProfileViewModel.kt` contains only `repository.toggleLoginState()` inside `PerformLogout` handling.
Manual target review confirmed `profile_store.cj::logout` performs only session logout through the preferences service.

featureContracts.featureName: Screen lifecycle, entry point initialization, resume/pause refresh, or teardown behavior.
featureContracts.sourceBehavior: `ProfileViewModel` has no independent load/resume teardown path; its lifecycle responsibility is limited to servicing the logout event while the profile screen is active.
featureContracts.targetRequiredBehavior: `bootstrap_page.cj::performLogout` owns the translated route teardown by clearing login and shell-local state before navigating to onboarding, while `resolveStartRoute(false)` preserves the post-logout entry behavior.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed `ProfileViewModel.kt` contains no initialization or resume logic beyond logout handling.
Manual target review confirmed `performLogout()` clears translated route-local state and `resolveStartRoute(false)` returns the onboarding route.

featureContracts.featureName: Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
featureContracts.sourceBehavior: `ProfileViewModel.onEvent` propagates the logout action by toggling login state and then invoking the navigation callback, so the profile screen reacts immediately to the logout event.
featureContracts.targetRequiredBehavior: `performLogout()` and `profile_store.cj::logout` preserve the same immediate session-update and route-handoff behavior, while `resolveStartRoute(false)` preserves the resulting onboarding entry state after logout.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed `ProfileViewModel.kt` performs a single logout state mutation followed by the supplied callback.
Manual target review confirmed `performLogout()` executes the translated logout mutation and immediate route transition without adding extra async state layers.
